skill.name: tech-doc
skill.description: A writing guide for clear, factual, and maintainable technical documentation in collaborative engineering work.
skill.summary: Create concise technical documentation with clear scope, evidence, and consistent Markdown structure.
skill.author: DCjanus

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed against SKILL.md and no executable behavior or malicious intent was found. The command alerts are Markdown inline-code examples, the weak-crypto alert has no supporting evidence on the cited line, and the entropy alert reflects readable non-English Markdown content.

low_findings.title: Dismissed static alert: Markdown inline code
low_findings.description: The three external command alerts at SKILL.md line 23 refer to inline Markdown text naming bash and sh code block labels. The file does not execute Ruby backticks or shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is prose that describes how to format command examples in Markdown. No executable script, Ruby code, or command substitution is present.

low_findings.title: Dismissed static alert: weak cryptographic algorithm
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alert at SKILL.md line 3 has no supporting evidence. The line is frontmatter text describing the documentation skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line contains a natural language description and no cryptographic API, algorithm name, or implementation. This is a pattern false positive.

low_findings.title: Dismissed static alert: obfuscation
low_findings.description: The high entropy alert at SKILL.md line 1 does not indicate obfuscation. The reviewed file is readable Markdown frontmatter and Chinese documentation guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found plain text Markdown rather than binary, encrypted, or encoded content. The entropy score is likely influenced by UTF-8 Chinese text.

content.value_statement: Technical teams often lose time when documentation lacks scope, evidence, and consistent structure. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to produce concise technical docs with clear assumptions and maintainable Markdown.
actual_capabilities: Structures explanatory documents with a clear goal, audience, scope, and outcome.
Guides quick reference and SOP pages using task scope, prerequisites, examples, and common errors.
Improves Markdown hierarchy, lists, tables, links, code blocks, and image alt text.
Preserves source meaning while improving wording, formatting, and annotations.
Encourages citation of authoritative sources, versions, and access dates.
Flags missing context, conflicts, and risky assumptions for human confirmation.
limitations: It does not verify facts unless reliable source material is provided.
It does not generate diagrams, screenshots, or external assets by itself.
It cannot replace subject matter review for complex engineering decisions.
It focuses on writing guidance, not automated publishing or repository changes.

use_cases.title: Prepare engineering design notes
use_cases.description: Turn rough implementation notes into a scoped explanation with assumptions, tradeoffs, and evidence.

use_cases.title: Standardize operations runbooks
use_cases.description: Create SOP pages with prerequisites, safe command examples, common errors, and related references.

use_cases.title: Improve product knowledge bases
use_cases.description: Rewrite help content into short sections with clear audience boundaries and stable Markdown formatting.

prompt_templates.title: Rewrite a rough technical note
prompt_templates.prompt: Rewrite this technical note for engineers. Start with the goal, audience, scope, and expected outcome. Keep claims limited to the source text: [paste note].

prompt_templates.title: Create a quick reference page
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a quick reference page for this task. Include scope, prerequisites, the shortest safe example, common parameters, common errors, and related references: [paste source].

prompt_templates.title: Review documentation for gaps
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this documentation for unclear scope, unsupported claims, missing citations, risky assumptions, and Markdown structure issues. Return prioritized fixes: [paste document].

prompt_templates.title: Build a maintainable SOP
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a maintainable SOP from these notes. Include prerequisites, permissions, safe defaults, rollback guidance, examples marked as examples, and open questions: [paste notes].

output_examples.input: A scattered set of notes about deploying a service.
output_examples.output: A short runbook with purpose, scope, prerequisites, safe deployment steps, verification checks, rollback notes, and unresolved questions.

output_examples.input: A long explanation of a new internal API.
output_examples.output: A concise technical overview with audience, key concepts, boundaries, request flow, known limitations, and cited source links.

output_examples.input: A draft README with mixed heading levels and unclear examples.
output_examples.output: A cleaner README structure with stable headings, focused lists, labeled examples, and notes for missing source evidence.

best_practices: Provide source notes, target audience, and scope before asking for a rewrite.
Ask the model to mark assumptions and open questions instead of filling gaps.
Review commands, values, and references with the responsible engineer before publishing.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to invent missing architecture details or performance numbers.
Do not publish generated SOPs without checking permissions and operational risk.
Do not mix tutorial, reference, and decision records in one unstructured page.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps write and revise technical documentation with clear scope, concise structure, and evidence-based claims.

faq.question: Can it write SOPs and runbooks?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides guidance for prerequisites, safe examples, common errors, and related references.

faq.question: Does it verify technical facts automatically?
faq.answer: No. It relies on the source material you provide and asks for confirmation when context is missing.

faq.question: Is it useful with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide Claude, Codex, and Claude Code when drafting or reviewing repository documentation.

faq.question: What input should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide rough notes, existing documentation, target readers, scope, references, and any required terminology.

faq.question: What should I review before publishing?
faq.answer: Check commands, permissions, data values, citations, and any assumptions marked for confirmation.
